column1: #mapping_set_description: Clinical_fields-level mappings from ncihtan/data-models@v25.2.1 to ncihtan/htan2-data-model@v1.3.0

column8: Identical field: HTAN ID SOP primary key for patient identification.
Direct match: both capture amino acid changes in variants.

Both classify tumor type with matching valid values and reference to caDSR:12922545 in the source comment. Confidence retained at 0.95 (not 1.0) because the justification was LogicalReasoning in the original; the caDSR match is noted in the comment but was not verified via DatabaseCrossReference matching. Justification updated to MappingReview to reflect this QA check.
Both indicate whether individual has consumed alcohol. Different caDSR IDs but semantically equivalent.
Both track follow-up timing. HTAN2 uses age in days; HTAN1 uses days from index.
Both track progression timing. Different temporal reference (age vs days from index).
Both track treatment end timing. Different temporal reference.
Both track treatment start timing. Different temporal reference.
Both capture reason for treatment cessation with overlapping value sets.
Both indicate whether any relative has cancer history with matching Yes/No values.

column1: data_models:Family History/Relatives with Cancer History Count
column8: Both capture count of relatives with cancer history.
Both capture smoking status with matching value sets. Different caDSR IDs but same concept.
Both capture treatment efficacy/response with matching value sets.
Both measure age at diagnosis. Target uses days; source uses years. Requires unit conversion.

column1: data_models:Diagnosis/Days to Last Known Disease Status
column2: Days to Last Known Disease Status
column8: Both measure time to last disease status. HTAN2 uses age in days; HTAN1 uses days from index date.

column1: data_models:Follow Up/Progression or Recurrence Anatomic Site
column8: Same concept. HTAN2 requires UBERON codes; HTAN1 uses text terms.
Same concept: treatment location. HTAN2 uses UBERON codes; HTAN1 uses text terms.

Corrected target from ClinicalData/VITAL_STATUS (a container reference) to VitalStatus/VITAL_STATUS (the actual field). Cross-class mapping: HTAN1 stores vital status in Demographics; HTAN2 has a dedicated VitalStatus class. closeMatch because the concept is the same but structural placement differs.

Structural container reference: Patient/Demographics in HTAN1 maps to the DEMOGRAPHICS reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels (HTAN1 component reference vs HTAN2 list slot), so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate rather than exactMatch.

Structural container reference: Patient/Diagnosis in HTAN1 maps to the DIAGNOSIS reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels, so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate rather than exactMatch.

Structural container reference: Patient/Exposure in HTAN1 maps to the EXPOSURES reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels (singular component reference vs plural list slot), so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate. Confidence reduced from 0.95 and predicate changed from exactMatch.

Structural container reference: Patient/Family History in HTAN1 maps to the FAMILY_HISTORY reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels, so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate rather than exactMatch.

Structural container reference: Patient/Follow Up in HTAN1 maps to the FOLLOW_UPS reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels (singular component reference vs plural list slot), so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate. Confidence reduced from 0.95 and predicate changed from exactMatch.

HTAN1 Gender (caDSR:2200604) was a conflated field covering both gender identity and biological sex. HTAN2 splits these into GENDER_IDENTITY and SEX. Gender→GENDER_IDENTITY is the primary conceptual match; confidence adjusted down from 0.9 to 0.8 to reflect that the source field was not exclusively a gender-identity field.

Structural container reference: Patient/Molecular Test in HTAN1 maps to the MOLECULAR_TESTS reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels (singular component reference vs plural list slot), so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate. Confidence reduced from 0.95 and predicate changed from exactMatch.

Structural container reference: Patient/Therapy in HTAN1 maps to the THERAPIES reference slot in HTAN2 ClinicalData. These operate at different structural levels (singular component reference vs plural list slot), so closeMatch at reduced confidence is appropriate. Confidence reduced from 0.95 and predicate changed from exactMatch.
Both identify anatomic site of disease origin. Target requires UBERON codes; source provides text terms.

Cross-class mapping: HTAN1 records progression/recurrence under Diagnosis; HTAN2 moved this to FollowUp. Name match is exact but structural placement differs, warranting closeMatch rather than exactMatch. Confidence reduced from 0.9 to 0.75 to reflect cross-class uncertainty.

Cross-class mapping: HTAN1 records progression/recurrence type under Diagnosis; HTAN2 moved this to FollowUp. Name match is exact but structural placement differs, warranting closeMatch rather than exactMatch. Confidence reduced from 0.9 to 0.75 to reflect cross-class uncertainty.

HTAN1 Gender (caDSR:2200604) was labeled 'Gender' not 'Sex', but the field conflated biological sex and gender identity. HTAN2 SEX is a narrower, more precise concept. The original field name and label suggest gender intent, so this is a broad mapping of the HTAN1 field to the biological-sex interpretation it also captured. Confidence adjusted down from 0.7 to 0.6 per the broadMatch guidance for this split.
Target is categorical result (Positive/Negative); source is numeric value. Related but different granularity.
Target is general environmental exposure; source is specific to tobacco smoke. Target is broader.
